questions.q: A fair coin is flipped 3 times. What is the probability of getting exactly 2 heads?
questions.explanation: Using the binomial formula: C(3,2) × (0.5)² × (0.5)¹ = 3 × 0.25 × 0.5 = 3/8. There are C(3,2)=3 ways to choose which 2 of 3 flips are heads (HHT, HTH, THH), each with probability (1/2)³ = 1/8. Total: 3 × 1/8 = 3/8.

questions.q: Bayes' theorem states P(A|B) equals what?
questions.explanation: Bayes' theorem: P(A|B) = P(B|A) × P(A) / P(B). P(B|A) is the likelihood, P(A) is the prior, P(B) is the marginal likelihood (normalizer). It allows updating a prior belief P(A) with new evidence B to get a posterior P(A|B). Fundamental to Bayesian statistics, spam filters, and medical diagnosis.
